perturbation.original_text: I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are not expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Employee the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by reversing the ownership of inventions. The original text assigns inventions to the Company, while the changed text assigns them to the Employee. This contradicts the section's initial statement and creates uncertainty about who owns the rights to any creations during employment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are not expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Employee the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions. I agree that I will promptly disclose to the Company any and all such Inventions, and that, upon request of the Company, I will execute and deliver any and all documents or instruments and take any other action which the Company shall deem necessary to assign to and vest completely in the Company, to perfect trademark, copyright and patent protection with respect to, or to otherwise protect the Company's trade secrets and proprietary interest in such Inventions. The obligations of this Section shall continue beyond the termination of my employment with respect to such Inventions conceived of or made by me during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text both state that inventions are considered "works for hire" and are assigned to the Company. The changed text states that inventions are 'not expressly regarded as works for hire' and assigns them to the Employee. This is a clear and direct conflict regarding ownership, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall not, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business."
perturbation.changed_text: While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business."
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by negating the non-compete clause. The 'not' has been removed, meaning the employee now agrees to engage in business activity competitive with PathNet, while also understanding that the original restrictions are to protect the company's interests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-COMPETITION COVENANT. While I am employed by the Company and for a period of two (2) years after the termination or cessation of such employment for any reason, I shall, without the Company's prior written consent, directly or indirectly, alone or as a partner, joint venturer, officer, director, employee, consultant, agent, independent contractor or stockholder of any company or business, engage in any business activity which is or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business." For purposes of this Agreement, the "PathNet Business" shall mean the business of installing, constructing, aggregating and linking digital capacity and marketing and selling the bulk 2 <PAGE> telecommunications capacity and services created by such systems. My ownership of not more than one(1%) percent of the shares of any class of stock of any corporation which is actively traded on a national securities exchange or on NASDAQ shall not be deemed, in and of itself, to violate the prohibitions of this paragraph. I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a non-compete clause, preventing the employee from engaging in activities competitive with PathNet. The changed text removes the 'not' from this clause, meaning the employee now agrees to engage in competitive activities. This directly contradicts the purpose and language of the 'NON-COMPETITION COVENANT' outlined in the Contradicted Text, which explicitly prohibits such activities to protect the company's interests. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will not encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, nor solicit nor hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company, unless first approached in writing by the Company employee.
perturbation.changed_text: During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, and solicit and hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company, unless first approached in writing by the Company employee.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation inverts the non-solicitation clause, creating an in-text contradiction. Now, the employee is obligated to solicit and hire employees, while the understanding is that the original section is for company protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-SOLICITATION. During my employment with the Company and for a period two (2) years thereafter, I will encourage any employee of the Company to terminate their employment with the Company, and solicit and hire any employee of the Company for employment by any corporation or any other commercial enterprise other than the Company, unless first approached in writing by the Company employee. I understand that the restrictions set forth in this Section are intended to protect the Company's valid business interests and agree that such restrictions are reasonable and appropriate for this purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains a non-solicitation clause preventing the employee from encouraging other employees to terminate their employment or soliciting/hiring them. The changed text completely inverts this clause, obligating the employee to encourage termination and solicit/hire employees. This directly contradicts the stated intent of the clause to protect the Company's business interests, as mentioned in the 'Contradicted Text', creating a strong in-text contradiction. The definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' applies here since different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will not receive any salary or benefits for any amount of time after such termination, with zero payments.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction by negating the severance clause. The original states salary will be provided, while the perturbation states no salary is provided. This creates uncertainty and disputes with severance policies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SEVERANCE. If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will not receive any salary or benefits for any amount of time after such termination, with zero payments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text specified a severance payment of $275,000, but the changed text explicitly states that no salary or benefits will be received after termination. This creates a direct inconsistency, as the changed text negates the original severance clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the perturbation explanation provided.
